title: Funky Crispy Uruguayan Chicken Frittata
description: A cozy, smoky‑cold, honey‑kissed frittata with crispy edges and Uruguayan flair, diabetic‑friendly.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F and spray a 6‑inch ramekin with olive oil.
In a bowl, toss chicken cubes with honey, smoked paprika, cumin, salt, and pepper until coated.
Heat a skillet over medium‑high and sear chicken for 4–5 minutes, until browned; remove and set aside.
In the same skillet, reduce heat to medium, add remaining oil, and sauté chopped cilantro for 1 minute.
Whisk eggs, almond milk, salt, and pepper until frothy, then stir in mozzarella, Parmesan, and cooked chicken.
Pour mixture into the prepared ramekin, smoothing the top with a spatula.
Bake for 18–20 minutes, until the frittata is set and edges are golden.
Remove from oven, drizzle additional honey over the top for a sweet glaze.
Let cool for 2 minutes, then slide the frittata onto a plate.
Garnish with extra cilantro and serve hot.
